title: daucus maritimus subsp. serratus
intro: Daucus maritimus subsp. serratus, commonly known as the coastal carrot or sea carrot, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carrot family, characterized by its distinctive yellow or white flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Daucus maritimus subsp. serratus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
